text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English. In this podcast, we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听Real Easy English。在这个播客节目中,我们用简单的英语进行真实对话,帮助大家学习。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie. Remember, you can watch a video version of this podcast on our website bbclearningenglish
zh: 我是Neil。我是Georgie。记得大家可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish

text: .com. How are you today,
zh: .com上观看这个播客的视频版本。你今天怎么样,

text: Georgie? I'm very well, thank you, today. Neil, how are you?
zh: Georgie?我今天很好,谢谢。Neil,你怎么样?

text: I'm OK. I've got one or two little worries. Have you?
zh: 我还行。我有一两件小烦恼。你呢?

text: Well, that's interesting because we are talking about worries today in this podcast.
zh: 嗯,这挺有意思的,因为我们今天这期播客要聊的正是烦恼这个话题。

text: Worries are problems that make you feel upset or scared.
zh: 烦恼是指让你感到难过或害怕的问题。

text: So, shall we start the conversation? Let's do it. So, Neil, you said you've got a couple of little worries. Do you want to share them?
zh: 那我们开始对话吧?来吧。Neil,你说你有一两个小烦恼,愿意分享一下吗?

text: Yeah. I think when you have kids, you're kind of slightly worried all the time.
zh: 好啊。我觉得当你有了孩子,你就会一直有点担心。

text: And Yesterday my son got hurt playing cricket
zh: 昨天我儿子打板球的时候受伤了,

text: and so this morning I just wanted to make sure he was feeling okay before he went to school.
zh: 所以今天早上我出门前想确认一下他感觉怎么样再去上学。

text: And how was he this morning? He seemed okay. Yeah? He seemed a bit bruised.
zh: 那他今天早上怎么样?看起来还好吗?他看起来有点淤青。

text: Oh no. An injury to the knee or something? Shoulder. Ouch.
zh: 哦不。是膝盖还是哪里受伤了?肩膀。哎呀。

text: And arm. Gosh. Yeah. And are you someone that worries a lot?
zh: 还有手臂。天哪。是啊。那你是一个经常担心的人吗?

text: No, not really. Sometimes, but I think there's lots of stuff that you can worry about
zh: 不算是。有时候会,但我觉得可以担心的事情太多了,

text: and if you worry about all of it, it can really affect you badly.
zh: 如果每件事都担心的话,真的会对你造成很不好的影响。

text: So I try, I know it's difficult sometimes, not to worry too much about things that aren't so important.
zh: 所以我尽量——虽然有时候挺难的——不去为那些不太重要的事情过分担心。

text: My thing is that worrying about a problem or a thing doesn't actually help.
zh: 我的想法是,担心一个问题或一件事其实并没有什么用。

text: So I'm a in general. I'm a person that doesn't worry too much.
zh: 所以总体来说,我是一个不太爱担心的人。

text: I kind of just think whatever happens so I'm generally quite chilled and relaxed and
zh: 我一般会想,无论发生什么都顺其自然,所以我整体上挺放松、挺淡定的,而且

text: Yeah, my friend always Says that to me because she's someone that worries a lot about everything
zh: 是的,我朋友经常这么跟我说,因为她是那种事事都担心的人,

text: travel You know all of those things and she always says how are you always so relaxed about things that go wrong, but?
zh: 比如旅行之类的所有事情,她总说,你怎么对那些出问题的事总是这么淡定?

text: You can't change you can't change them. So that's true That's a very healthy attitude probably.
zh: 你没法改变,你没法改变它们。这倒是真的。这可能是一种很健康的心态。

text: So I don't tend to worry a lot, but you worry a little bit because you have kids.
zh: 所以我不太容易担心,但你因为有孩子,会有一点担心。

text: What kinds of things do you do to make yourself feel more relaxed?
zh: 那你会做些什么来让自己放松一点呢?

text: Well, I think like you said, one useful thing to do
zh: 嗯,我觉得就像你说的,一个有用的方法

text: could be to try to be rational.
zh: 可能就是尽量理性一点,

text: So to think that what you're worried about probably
zh: 想想你担心的事情很可能

text: isn't that important or likely to happen.
zh: 并没有那么重要,或者根本不太可能发生。

text: Yeah, so you're quite chilled, as you said. That must be nice for your friends.
zh: 是啊,就像你说的,你挺淡定的。你的朋友们一定觉得跟你在一起很舒服。

text: Yes. And if you have, like you said, a friend who worries about everything, how do you help them?
zh: 是的。如果像你说的那样,有个朋友什么事都担心,你会怎么帮她?

text: Finding maybe a distraction, like something that makes you feel good, good,
zh: 也许可以找点让自己分心的事,做一些让你感觉良好的事情,

text: like a TV program that you love or seeing friends that love you that can make you feel more relaxed.
zh: 比如看一部你喜欢的电视节目,或者见见爱你的朋友,这些都能让你更放松。

text: Mindfulness techniques as well, maybe jigsaws. I've talked about puzzles in the past.
zh: 正念练习也不错,或许还可以拼拼图。我以前也说过拼图这个话题。

text: Do you have any other tips? No, these are all very good ideas.
zh: 你还有别的建议吗?没有了,这些都是很好的主意。

text: Thank you. Yeah, I think it can be useful
zh: 谢谢。是的,我觉得有一些能让你分心的事情会很有用,

text: to have something that distracts you, like maybe drawing, listening to music, playing an instrument can
zh: 比如画画、听音乐、演奏乐器,

text: help. Something that just kind of takes that time away. It takes your mind off the stress.
zh: 都能帮上忙。就是找点事情让自己暂时抽离出来,把注意力从压力上转移开。

text: let's recap the vocabulary we heard during the conversation, starting with worry.
zh: 我们来回顾一下这次对话中出现的词汇,先从worry这个词开始。

text: A worry Worry is a problem that makes you feel upset or scared.
zh: worry(担心/烦恼)是指让你感到难过或害怕的问题。

text: And worry is also a verb. So, for example, I worry about my kids.
zh: worry也可以作动词用。比如说,I worry about my kids(我担心我的孩子)。

text: Yes, and we also use the adjective worried to describe that feeling of being scared or upset about something.
zh: 没错,我们还会用形容词worried来形容那种因为某事而感到害怕或难过的心情。

text: For example, I'm worried about climate change.
zh: 比如说,I'm worried about climate change(我担心气候变化)。

text: And we heard the expression take your mind off something, which means to do something to stop you worrying.
zh: 我们还听到了take your mind off something这个表达,意思是做某事来让自己不再为某事担心。

text: For example, I play my guitar to take my mind off my worries.
zh: 比如说,I play my guitar to take my mind off my worries(我弹吉他来让自己不去想那些烦恼)。

text: We also heard chilled, which is another word for relaxed or calm, and it's quite an informal word.
zh: 我们还听到了chilled这个词,是relaxed或calm的另一种说法,是一个比较口语化的词。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English. Try the worksheet on our website to test what you've learned.
zh: Real Easy English这一期节目就到这里。去我们网站上试试练习题,看看你学得怎么样。

text: Go to bbclearningenglish .com. See you then. Goodbye.
zh: 请访问bbclearningenglish.com。我们下次再见。再见。
